summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a legendary 9/14/82 show with a smoking Space→Aiko jam, killer China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition and hot Jerry Garcia playing.
review: Fans rave about the high‑energy second set – especially the Space into Aiko jam, the seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition, and a standout High Time. The SBD tape is generally clear with only typical early‑’80s hiss and occasional crackle, and Phil’s bass comes through unusually well for this era.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 reviews overwhelmingly positive (average ★4.3), with consistent praise for both performance and recording; only minor technical complaints.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear SBD tape, a bit of hiss/crackle (“bacon frying” comment) but overall balanced mix; Phil’s bass is surprisingly audible for an ’82 board, occasional mic bleed on drums.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical early‑80s layout – long first set, then a second set that kicks off with China Cat → Rider, followed by the iconic Space→Aiko jam and six songs before drums; the pacing keeps the energy simmering and builds to a hot climax.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is described as sweet and “hot” throughout; his lead on the Aiko/Space jam and High Time receives high praise.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is louder than usual for an ’82 board; noted smoking a stage monitor after Rider, showing his presence onstage.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s vocals and rhythm work shine in the Space→Aiko jam; his spoken “we’re gonna rock on outta here” line is highlighted.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s keyboard work gets special mention, especially during China Cat Sunflower and its transition into Rider.
